analysis.reasoning: Core message: An informational post announcing an exhibition on anti-Nazi resistance in Europe and an online conference organized by the Russian Historical Society. Evaluation per narrative: RU1 (West immoral/hostile): Not present; the tweet does not criticize the West or allies. RU2 (Russia leads alternative system): Not present; no claim about alternative multilateralism or non-Western cooperation. RU3 (Russia benevolent partner): Not explicit; while it promotes a Russian-organized event, it does not frame Russia as aiding peace or development. RU4 (Russian history is admirable): Partially aligned; it highlights a history-focused event by a Russian institution, but the content concerns European anti-Nazi resistance broadly, not specifically extolling Russian historical achievements. Insufficient to claim admiration of Russian history. RU5 (Russia at the world’s forefront): Not present; no claims of current successes. RU6 (Russia interesting culturally): Not applicable; this is not cultural-tourism promotion. Conclusion: No listed strategic narrative is clearly promoted.
